fault_hypothesis: The condition on line 74 uses strict greater-than (`>`) instead of greater-than-or-equal (`>=`), so when `len(active) == limit` the gate incorrectly allows the upload instead of blocking it.

constraints.intent: When len(active) equals limit the condition `len(active) > limit` is False, so the gate stays open — the condition should be `>=` to block at exactly the limit.
In the else branch (gate open), the number of active uploads must be strictly less than the limit; if it equals the limit the gate should have been closed.
Sanity check that active count is compared correctly against limit before the branch decision is made.
Active timestamps must be a non-negative subset of all timestamps, confirming filtering is correct before the limit comparison.
